title: Trump Says US 'No Longer Needs NATO' — Reports of Article 5 'Pay-to-Play' Proposal Emerge
detail: Trump's Miami forum remarks rippled across allied capitals on Saturday. Political Wire reported: 'Trump Says the U.S. No Longer Needs NATO.' GMA News headlined: 'Trump says we don\'t have to be there for NATO.' Simultaneously, The Telegraph reported the US is actively considering a 'pay-to-play' model that would strip Article 5 protections from NATO members that fail to reach the 5% GDP defense spending target — barring non-compliant states from joint exercises, decisions on alliance expansion, and invocation of the mutual defense clause. Multiple outlets confirmed US government sourcing on the proposal. EADaily reported Trump 'spoke about the possible termination of US support for NATO.' Analysts described the cumulative effect as potentially 'shattering' the alliance in ways Trump's earlier skepticism had only 'shaken,' as the Iran war transformed rhetorical pressure into a concrete dispute over US obligations.

media.caption: Trump says US no longer needs NATO, pay-to-play proposal emerges

title: Pentagon Redirects $750M From NATO-Ukraine Arms Program to Replenish US Stocks
detail: The Pentagon notified Congress of plans to redirect approximately $750 million from the NATO arms supply program for Ukraine to replenish US military stocks — a further concrete signal of US deprioritization of NATO commitments in the context of the Iran war. The move was widely interpreted in European capitals as a deliberate demonstration that the US intended to rebalance its strategic resources away from European-linked commitments. It reinforced the European push to accelerate the SAFE instrument and EU ReArm Europe plan as insurance against US disengagement. Polish and Baltic officials expressed concern that the redirection could affect resupply pipelines they depended on.

media.caption: Pentagon redirects $750M from NATO-Ukraine arms program to replenish US stocks
